questions.text: What is the reflection of the point (3, -4) across the x-axis?
questions.hint: When reflecting across the x-axis, the x-coordinate stays the same and the y-coordinate changes sign.
questions.solution: Reflecting (x, y) across the x-axis results in (x, -y). Therefore, (3, -4) becomes (3, 4).

questions.text: Find the distance from the point (-5, 12) to the origin.
questions.hint: Use the distance formula from the origin: √(x² + y²)
